request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the CLARITY Act be signed into law in 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (20 events):
- 2026-05-19 [A] CFTC Sues Minnesota to Block State Law — CFTC suing Minnesota shows federal assertion over state crypto rules, modestly increasing odds Congress favors a federal CLARITY Act (bullish).
- 2026-05-18 [background] Rescission of Policy Regarding Denials in Settlements of Enforcement Actions — Securities and Exchange Commission — U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-05-13 [background] CME | Expansion of the Trading and ClearingHours for all Cryptocurrency Futures and Options on Futures Contracts. | [1] — CFTC expanding crypto futures trading hours underscores an active CFTC role in crypto markets, mildly increasing odds Congress favors a CFTC-friendly CLARITY Act.
- 2026-05-07 [background] GMNI - Liquidity Incentive Program - 05/07/2026 — CFTC action involving Gemini is tangentially relevant to jurisdictional implementation debates but doesn't materially change chances H.R.3633 will become law.
- 2026-05-06 [background] CDNA - Amendments to rules on Insider definition, jurisdiction… - 05/06/2026 — CFTC rule amendments reflect agency activity on crypto jurisdiction but are background regulatory action, not direct evidence of H.R.3633 being passed/signed.
- 2026-05-01 [background] 05.01.2026 - The changes amend Rules related to Bitnomial's non-FCM Clearing Members - BTNL — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-04-28 [background] CFTC Sues Wisconsin to Reaffirm its Exclusive Jurisdiction Over Prediction Markets — CFTC suing to assert exclusive jurisdiction over prediction markets bolsters its claim over digital markets, modestly increasing H.R.3633's chance (bullish).
- 2026-04-17 [background] GMNI Disclosure about AI information Features available to Members — CFTC disclosure about Gemini AI features shows active regulator involvement in crypto but is background noise that doesn't materially affect H.R.3633 passage odds.
- 2026-04-17 [background] 13th Annual Conference on Financial Market Regulation — SEC conference on market regulation signals regulator engagement but provides no clear change to H.R.3633's chances of becoming law.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Crypto Market Maker Program - 04/16/2026 — CFTC launching a Coinbase crypto market-maker program signals regulatory activity but doesn't materially change likelihood H.R.3633 is enacted.
- 2026-04-16 [background] COIN | Modifications to implement hard limits… - 04/16/2026 — CFTC rule change on trading limits (COIN) is an agency action unrelated to Congress passing/signing H.R.3633; provides only minor background context.
- 2026-04-13 [A] Staff Statement Regarding Broker-Dealer Registration of Certain User Interfaces Utilized to Prepare Transactions in Crypto Asset Securities — SEC staff asserting broker-dealer registration for crypto UIs likely increases pressure for Congress to enact clarifying law, raising odds H.R.3633 passes.
- 2026-04-10 [background] NPRM: Anti-Money Laundering and CFT Programs - 10.04.2026 — FinCEN's AML/CFT NPRM shapes the digital-asset regulatory backdrop but does not directly affect whether H.R.3633 is passed and signed; low-impact background signal.
- 2026-04-08 [background] Administrative Summary - 2026-04-08 - Francis Decker, CPA — Securities and Exchange Commission — Accounting and Auditing Enforcement — U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-03-23 [A] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets — SEC guidance asserting securities coverage of crypto weakens the case for shifting spot jurisdiction to the CFTC, lowering odds that H.R.3633 passes.
- 2026-03-18 [A] Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets and Certain Transactions Involving Crypto Assets Corporation Finance — SEC guidance applying securities laws to crypto reduces congressional pressure to pass the CLARITY Act, lowering its passage odds.
- 2026-01-29 [background] COIN | Modifications to the Non-Crypto Market Maker Program — U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-01-23 [background] CME - Implementation of the Chainlink, Stellar, & Cardano Futures Market Maker Program — Commodity Futures Trading Commission — Commodity Futures Trading Commission: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2026-01-09 [background] Secretary Bessent Announces Initiatives to Combat Rampant Fraud in Minnesota — Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) — Financial Crimes Enforcement Network: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2025-12-08 [background] Acting Chairman Pham Announces Launch of Digital Assets Pilot Program for Tokenized Collateral in Derivatives Markets — CFTC pilot shows regulator activity on digital assets but is not direct evidence for or against H.R.3633 becoming law.

response.text: SEC moves in March 2026 to apply securities laws to crypto dampen momentum for shifting jurisdiction, but an April staff statement on broker-dealer registration for crypto UIs revives pressure for statutory clarity. Concurrent CFTC actions, including suits against WI and MN and market-expanding steps, nudge Congress toward a CFTC-friendly CLARITY Act. Bill passage and presidential signature remain pending.
